title: LiveExploreTargets
description: Output of Step 7's live-explore-target-extractor pre-pass: the concrete UI pages/features a test design exercises, plus how a user reaches each. Recovers targets semantically from prose/journey test designs that name no URL paths (where the lexical route regex finds nothing). Consumed by s07_live_explore to build the site-explorer's TESTED TARGETS checklist and to size its turn budget. Non-authoritative grounding context, not a hand-off artifact.

targets.description: The tested UI surfaces (pages/dialogs/panels). One entry per distinct screen; duplicates merged.

name.description: Human name of the tested page/feature, in the app's own terms where possible (e.g. 'My Notifications inbox').

reach_via.description: Shortest realistic user navigation path to the target, in the app's own nav/menu/button labels (e.g. 'My Pages menu -> My Notifications'). May be empty when the target is the app root.

why.description: One short phrase tying the target to what the tests do there.

routes.description: URL paths the test design states EXPLICITLY (may be empty). Never fabricated from a feature name.
